dsl_rule.translation_notes: Narrow-range detection modeled using bb_bandwidth (Bollinger Band width as volatility compression proxy) below 0.02. Range expansion threshold approximated using range > atr_14 (represents >100% ATR expansion). Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) used to detect recent range breakout with implicit confirmation via close crossing these levels. Entry is directional (LONG on breakout above; SHORT on breakout below). Stop loss set at 1.5x ATR equivalent via stop_loss_pct. Lookback period implicitly captured in Donchian and ATR calculations.

backtest_rule.reason: Narrow-range detection modeled using bb_bandwidth (Bollinger Band width as volatility compression proxy) below 0.02. Range expansion threshold approximated using range > atr_14 (represents >100% ATR expansion). Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) used to detect recent range breakout with implicit confirmation via close crossing these levels. Entry is directional (LONG on breakout above; SHORT on breakout below). Stop loss set at 1.5x ATR equivalent via stop_loss_pct. Lookback period implicitly captured in Donchian and ATR calculations.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter in the direction of a range expansion in a previously narrow-range market, as the expansion signals the market is committed to moving in that direction.
